# Example Bakery — full content

The complete text of our website, gathered under headings so answer engines can read it without JavaScript.

## Our history

Example Bakery opened in downtown Springfield in 1998 with one small oven and a single jar of sourdough starter. From the very first week, we baked every loaf by hand, early each morning, and we still do that today. The recipe has barely changed over the years, because we believe good bread needs time, simple grain and patience. What started as a tiny counter on Main Street slowly grew into the neighbourhood bakery you know now. We added croissants and seasonal tarts in the mid two thousands, and later began taking custom cake orders. Through every season, the same family has shaped the dough, watched the ovens and greeted each regular by name.

Example Bakery opened in downtown Springfield in 1998 with one small oven and a single jar of sourdough starter. From the very first week, we baked every loaf by hand, early each morning, and we still do that today. The recipe has barely changed over the years, because we believe good bread needs time, simple grain and patience. What started as a tiny counter on Main Street slowly grew into the neighbourhood bakery you know now. We added croissants and seasonal tarts in the mid two thousands, and later began taking custom cake orders. Through every season, the same family has shaped the dough, watched the ovens and greeted each regular by name.

Example Bakery opened in downtown Springfield in 1998 with one small oven and a single jar of sourdough starter. From the very first week, we baked every loaf by hand, early each morning, and we still do that today. The recipe has barely changed over the years, because we believe good bread needs time, simple grain and patience. What started as a tiny counter on Main Street slowly grew into the neighbourhood bakery you know now. We added croissants and seasonal tarts in the mid two thousands, and later began taking custom cake orders. Through every season, the same family has shaped the dough, watched the ovens and greeted each regular by name.

## How we bake sourdough

Good bread takes time, not shortcuts, and our sourdough is proof of that simple idea. We mix flour, water and salt, then let the dough rest overnight so the wild yeast can work. The long, slow rise gives the crust its deep colour and the crumb its open, airy texture. We shape each loaf by hand, score the top with a blade, and bake it in a hot stone oven. A jar of starter sits at the heart of the process, and we feed it fresh flour every single day. Because the starter is alive, no two bakes are ever exactly alike, and that is part of the charm.

Good bread takes time, not shortcuts, and our sourdough is proof of that simple idea. We mix flour, water and salt, then let the dough rest overnight so the wild yeast can work. The long, slow rise gives the crust its deep colour and the crumb its open, airy texture. We shape each loaf by hand, score the top with a blade, and bake it in a hot stone oven. A jar of starter sits at the heart of the process, and we feed it fresh flour every single day. Because the starter is alive, no two bakes are ever exactly alike, and that is part of the charm.

Good bread takes time, not shortcuts, and our sourdough is proof of that simple idea. We mix flour, water and salt, then let the dough rest overnight so the wild yeast can work. The long, slow rise gives the crust its deep colour and the crumb its open, airy texture. We shape each loaf by hand, score the top with a blade, and bake it in a hot stone oven. A jar of starter sits at the heart of the process, and we feed it fresh flour every single day. Because the starter is alive, no two bakes are ever exactly alike, and that is part of the charm.

## Croissants and pastries

Our butter croissants are rolled and folded by hand, layer upon layer, until they turn light and flaky. We laminate the dough with cold butter, then rest it, fold it again, and repeat until it is ready. Fresh croissants come out of the oven each morning, and they sell out quickly on the weekend. Alongside croissants we bake pain au chocolat, almond twists and small seasonal fruit tarts. The fruit for our tarts comes from farms in the region, so the flavour changes with the calendar. Everything in the pastry case is made on the day you buy it, never frozen and never held over.

Our butter croissants are rolled and folded by hand, layer upon layer, until they turn light and flaky. We laminate the dough with cold butter, then rest it, fold it again, and repeat until it is ready. Fresh croissants come out of the oven each morning, and they sell out quickly on the weekend. Alongside croissants we bake pain au chocolat, almond twists and small seasonal fruit tarts. The fruit for our tarts comes from farms in the region, so the flavour changes with the calendar. Everything in the pastry case is made on the day you buy it, never frozen and never held over.

Our butter croissants are rolled and folded by hand, layer upon layer, until they turn light and flaky. We laminate the dough with cold butter, then rest it, fold it again, and repeat until it is ready. Fresh croissants come out of the oven each morning, and they sell out quickly on the weekend. Alongside croissants we bake pain au chocolat, almond twists and small seasonal fruit tarts. The fruit for our tarts comes from farms in the region, so the flavour changes with the calendar. Everything in the pastry case is made on the day you buy it, never frozen and never held over.

## Custom cakes

We began taking custom cake orders in 2012, and they have become a much loved part of the shop. For birthdays, weddings and anniversaries, we bake to order and decorate each cake by hand. You can choose the sponge, the filling and the finish, and we will talk through the details with you. We ask for a few days of notice on large cakes, so we can plan the bake around the daily bread. Every cake is made with the same butter, eggs and flour we use for the rest of our counter. If you are not sure what you want, come in and taste a slice, and we will help you decide.

We began taking custom cake orders in 2012, and they have become a much loved part of the shop. For birthdays, weddings and anniversaries, we bake to order and decorate each cake by hand. You can choose the sponge, the filling and the finish, and we will talk through the details with you. We ask for a few days of notice on large cakes, so we can plan the bake around the daily bread. Every cake is made with the same butter, eggs and flour we use for the rest of our counter. If you are not sure what you want, come in and taste a slice, and we will help you decide.

We began taking custom cake orders in 2012, and they have become a much loved part of the shop. For birthdays, weddings and anniversaries, we bake to order and decorate each cake by hand. You can choose the sponge, the filling and the finish, and we will talk through the details with you. We ask for a few days of notice on large cakes, so we can plan the bake around the daily bread. Every cake is made with the same butter, eggs and flour we use for the rest of our counter. If you are not sure what you want, come in and taste a slice, and we will help you decide.

## Ingredients and sourcing

Our flour comes from mills in the region, and we name the farms on our labels so you know the source. We use unbleached flour, real butter, free range eggs and sea salt, with nothing artificial added. Buying local grain keeps the supply chain short and supports the farmers who live near the shop. When fruit is in season, we buy it from nearby orchards and use it while it is at its best. We keep the ingredient list short on purpose, because simple bread made well needs very little. If you have an allergy or a question about what is in a product, please ask and we will tell you.

Our flour comes from mills in the region, and we name the farms on our labels so you know the source. We use unbleached flour, real butter, free range eggs and sea salt, with nothing artificial added. Buying local grain keeps the supply chain short and supports the farmers who live near the shop. When fruit is in season, we buy it from nearby orchards and use it while it is at its best. We keep the ingredient list short on purpose, because simple bread made well needs very little. If you have an allergy or a question about what is in a product, please ask and we will tell you.

Our flour comes from mills in the region, and we name the farms on our labels so you know the source. We use unbleached flour, real butter, free range eggs and sea salt, with nothing artificial added. Buying local grain keeps the supply chain short and supports the farmers who live near the shop. When fruit is in season, we buy it from nearby orchards and use it while it is at its best. We keep the ingredient list short on purpose, because simple bread made well needs very little. If you have an allergy or a question about what is in a product, please ask and we will tell you.

## Visiting the bakery

You will find Example Bakery at 1 Main Street, in the heart of downtown Springfield. Our doors open Tuesday through Sunday, from seven in the morning until seven at night. We close on Mondays, which is when we clean the ovens and feed the starter for the week ahead. There is seating by the window if you would like to enjoy a coffee and a pastry in the shop. Parking is easy on the side streets, and the bus stops just around the corner from our door. Fresh bread arrives on the shelf through the morning, so there is almost always a warm loaf ready.

You will find Example Bakery at 1 Main Street, in the heart of downtown Springfield. Our doors open Tuesday through Sunday, from seven in the morning until seven at night. We close on Mondays, which is when we clean the ovens and feed the starter for the week ahead. There is seating by the window if you would like to enjoy a coffee and a pastry in the shop. Parking is easy on the side streets, and the bus stops just around the corner from our door. Fresh bread arrives on the shelf through the morning, so there is almost always a warm loaf ready.

We close on Mondays, which is when we clean the ovens and feed the starter for the week ahead. There is seating by the window if you would like to enjoy a coffee and a pastry in the shop. Parking is easy on the side streets, and the bus stops just around the corner from our door. Fresh bread arrives on the shelf through the morning, so there is almost always a warm loaf ready.

## Ordering online

Fresh bread sells out fast on the weekend, so an online order holds your loaf until you arrive. Ordering ahead also helps us plan the bake, which means we waste less flour and less time. You can place an order the night before and pick it up the next morning at the counter. Same day pickup is available for regulars who order early, before the first bake leaves the oven. If you need to change or cancel an order, please call the shop and we will sort it out for you. We answer the phone during shop hours, and we are always happy to help a first time customer.

Fresh bread sells out fast on the weekend, so an online order holds your loaf until you arrive. Ordering ahead also helps us plan the bake, which means we waste less flour and less time. You can place an order the night before and pick it up the next morning at the counter. Same day pickup is available for regulars who order early, before the first bake leaves the oven. If you need to change or cancel an order, please call the shop and we will sort it out for you. We answer the phone during shop hours, and we are always happy to help a first time customer.

If you need to change or cancel an order, please call the shop and we will sort it out for you. We answer the phone during shop hours, and we are always happy to help a first time customer.

## Contact and community

You can reach the bakery by phone at plus one, five five five, zero one zero zero during shop hours. We are proud to be part of this town, and we support local schools, markets and small charities. Stop by, say hello, and taste what a slow rise and a little patience can really do for a loaf. Follow the seasons with us, from summer fruit tarts to the warm spiced breads of the winter. Whether you come for a daily loaf or a birthday cake, you are always welcome at our counter. Thank you for reading, and thank you for supporting a small family bakery in Springfield.

You can reach the bakery by phone at plus one, five five five, zero one zero zero during shop hours. We are proud to be part of this town, and we support local schools, markets and small charities.
